name: Harlequin
flavor: The maddest act under the big top: a Joker who stops treating melee as something to avoid and starts treating a ring full of enemies as the best possible stage, grinning wider the closer the danger gets.

features.name: Reckless Grin
features.description: As a bonus action, spend 1 Mayhem to throw caution to the wind until the start of your next turn: you have advantage on melee attack rolls you make with a finesse weapon you're proficient with, but attack rolls against you also have advantage until the start of your next turn. You can only be under the effect of Reckless Grin once at a time; using it again while it is already active changes nothing and doesn't cost additional Mayhem.

features.name: Center of the Ring
features.description: You thrive surrounded by danger. At the start of your turn, if you are within 5 feet of at least two hostile creatures, you gain temporary hit points equal to your {{Charisma modifier (min 1)|set from your stats on your character sheet}}, and for the rest of the turn moving through a space occupied by a hostile creature costs you no extra movement (you still can't end your movement there). As with temporary hit points from any other source, these don't stack — if you already have some, keep whichever total is higher.

features.name: Jester's License
features.description: The fool may say what the courtier cannot. When you frame it as performance — a jest, a song, a pantomime — you can deliver insults, accusations, and uncomfortable truths without your audience turning violent: creatures may eject you, despise you, or plot quietly, but they don't move to harm you for the words alone. You also have advantage on Charisma (Performance) checks, and on Charisma (Persuasion) checks made while playing the fool.
features.sheetSummary: Insults/accusations/hard truths delivered as performance don't provoke violence (targets may still eject or resent you). Advantage on Performance checks and on Persuasion while playing the fool.
